violations.description: Test asserts recency against DateTimeOffset.UtcNow instead of a fixed clock.
violations.suggestion: Inject or expose TimeProvider for the generated timestamp and assert against FakeTimeProvider.GetUtcNow().

violations.description: Dead-letter timestamp assertions use a current-time tolerance window.
violations.suggestion: Drive dead-letter creation with a fixed TimeProvider or assert relative ordering captured before the operation.

violations.description: Bunit test uses Thread.Sleep(50) to flush possible renders.
violations.suggestion: Use bUnit WaitForState/WaitForAssertion or a render-count probe that observes the actual condition.

violations.description: File lock retry loops block the test thread with Thread.Sleep.
violations.suggestion: Convert retry helpers to async and await Task.Delay(..., TestContext.Current.CancellationToken).

violations.description: Disabled collector test still uses a fixed 100 ms delay.
violations.suggestion: Replace with an observable completion signal or call ExecuteAsync through a testable wrapper.

recommendations: Continue the successful FakeTimeProvider pattern now present in DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests.
Remove wall-clock assertion windows from actor/dead-letter tests.
Convert blocking Thread.Sleep retry loops to cancellable async waits.
summary: The prior 51-second collector delay finding is resolved. Remaining determinism risk is concentrated in wall-clock assertions and three small hard-wait sites.
